name: Adams, Fred
motto: "Oh, Jupiter, how weary are my spirits."

name: Boyett, Ruben Watson
motto: "As mild a mannered man as ever scuttled a ship, or cut a throat."

name: Breland, Jesse Josiah
motto: "As proper a man as one may see in a summer's day."

name: Brown, Rufus Lee
motto: "Blessings be on him who first invented sleep."

name: Brown, Andrew Jackson
motto: "The hand that follows intellect can achieve."
description: Completed Course in Three-Year Study; Student Assistant in Educational Department.

name: Burks, Joseph Lee
motto: "Had he been caught when young he might have been tamed."

name: Burks, Rutherford Bernard
motto: "Thy face is the index of a noble mind."

name: Carpenter, James M.
motto: "He is complete in feature and in mind With all good grace to grace a gentleman."

name: Lane, Terrell Chandler
motto: "Whoever excels in what we prize Appears a hero in our eyes."

name: John, R. Dinsmore
motto: "I have nobody; I am in love with the world."

name: Harry, M. Dobrowski
motto: "I'll have my bond."

name: Paul, Edward Ford
motto: "Over books I consume the midnight oil."

name: Furr, Hallie May
motto: "Knowledge and truth and virtue were her theme."

name: Furr, Stacey
motto: "Thy modesty is a candle to thy merit."

name: Gautier, Harold White
motto: "Must I work? Oh, what a waste of time."

name: Green, Emmeline
motto: "Many daughters have done virtuously, but thou excellest them all."
clubs: Social Secretary Y. W. C. A., 1912-13

name: Good, Robert Mcgowan
motto: "If honor gives greatness, he's great as a king."

name: Hardage, Robert Henry
motto: "Watch Tom Hardage."

name: Holloway, Ernest Duncan
motto: "A jolly good fellow, with all of the virtues and none of the faults."
clubs: Secretary and Treasurer Red and Blue Club

name: Holloway, Paul Dickson
motto: "Oh, why should life all labor be?"

name: Jones, Thomas Daniel
motto: "Perseverance conquers all things."

name: Hood, David Spurgeon
motto: "And what is thy history? A blank, my lord."

name: Kincannon, Jim Clayte Jr.
motto: "Heaven bless thee, Thou hast the sweetest face I ever looked on."

name: Leavell, Roland Quinchie
motto: "His life was gentle, and the elements so mixed in him that nature might stand up and say to all the world 'This is a man!'"
clubs: Delegate to Y. M. C. A. Convention at Millsaps, 1909

name: Leftwich, George Jabez Jr.
motto: "O wad some power the giftie gie us To see oursel's as others see us."

name: Moss, Mary Elizabeth
motto: "What sweet delight a quiet life affords."

name: Nicholson, Ruby
motto: "Work, courage, honor, these indeed your sustenance and birthright are."
clubs: Chairman Inter-Collegiate Committee, Y. W. C. A., 1911-12
Delegate Y. W. C. A. Conference, Columbus, 1911
Delegate Y. W. C. A. Conference, Black Mountain, N. C., 1912

name: Pegues, Sam Hill
motto: "The heroes are not all six feet tall. Large souls may dwell in bodies small."

name: Picard, Florence
motto: "A heart to conceive, an understanding to direct, and hands to execute."

name: Powe, Alexander McKee
motto: "Men may come and men may go, But I stay on forever."

name: Rawls, Fred
motto: "The love of fame is the last weakness which even the wise resign."

name: Riley, John Powell
motto: "Go not indiscriminately to classes."

name: Smith, Dudley C.
motto: "Fraught with learning."

name: Rosensweig, Maurice Lee
motto: "The mildest manners, the gentlest heart."

name: Sowell, Julia
motto: "She has a nature that is gentle and refined."

name: Spence, John Lee Jr.
motto: "Manhood fused with female grace."

name: Stephens, John Ellett
motto: "Whosoever findeth a wife, findeth a good thing."

name: Stevens, W. R. Barksdale
motto: "Let's have wine, woman, mirth and laughter, Sermons and classes some day after."

name: Tanner, Benton MacArthur
motto: "Oh, wise and most learned doctor."

name: Watts, Reece Robert
motto: "The noblest Roman of them all."

name: Backstrom, James Lawrence
motto: "His smile doth beam, Even as the sun upon a summer's day."

name: Brewer, Edward Cage
motto: "So wise, so grave, of so perplexed a tongue, and so loud withal that would not wag nor scarce lie still without a fee."
clubs: Treasurer of Y. M. C. A., 1913-1914
Delegate International Student Volunteer Convention, Kansas City, 1914
Winner of Law Books Given by Southern Bench and Bar
Represented Ole Miss in the Ole Miss-A. & M. Debate, 1911

name: Churchwell, Wyatt Clinton
motto: "Along the cool sequestered vale of life— He keeps the noiseless tenor of his way."

name: Clark, Arthur Barnett
motto: "Not over serious, not too gay, But a rare good fellow."

name: Dorrah, Clinton Edwards
motto: "And when a lady's in the case, You know all other things give place."
clubs: Y. M. C. A. Publicity Committee, 1913-1914

name: Doxey, Wall
motto: "A voice more thrilling ne'er was heard In Springtime from the cuckoo bird."
major: LL.B., B. S. U. of M., 1913
clubs: Member of Winning Inter-Society Debating Team

name: Elam, Thomas Hicks
motto: "Now, in the name of all the gods at once, Upon what meat doth this our Caesar feed That he is grown so great?"

name: Franklin, Cornell Sidney
motto: "I want to grow as beautiful as God meant me to be."

name: Geisenberger, Wilfred Alexander
motto: "Fire in each eye, and papers in each hand, He roves, recites and maddens 'round the land."

name: Green, Walter Galloway
motto: "Be gone, my cases, I give you to the wind."
clubs: Social Committee Y. M. C. A.

name: Holt, Horatio Ogden
motto: "A better fellow to walk with, down life's highway, is seldom found."

name: James, Jerome Parker
motto: "A man in whom there dwells all that is honest and honorable."
major: LL.B., B. S., U. of M., 1913

name: Johnston, Henry Grady
motto: "I dare do all that may become a man, Who dares do more is none."
clubs: Vice President Y. M. C. A., 1912-1914
First Assistant Business Manager Varsity Voice, 1910-1911

name: Livingston, Edward Martin
motto: "What a fine man hath thy tailor made thee."

name: Long, Sam Holden
motto: "If hot air were music I would be a whole brass band."

name: Shelton, Allen Dupree
motto: "They say thou art a lawyer in disguise, 'Tis well, I would not suspect it otherwise."

name: Smith, Fred B.
motto: "The finest fruit earth holds up to its Maker is a finished man."
clubs: Secretary and Treasurer of Scribblers' Club, 1913-1914
Y. M. C. A. Bible Study Committee, 1913-1914

name: Sumners, James Warren
motto: "I am as sober as a judge."
clubs: Vice President Blackstone Club for two terms
Winner of Prize from "Southern Bench and Bar Review" for Article on "Grandfather Clause"
President of Directors of Student Co-Op Boarding Club

name: Sutherland, Hugh Lewis
motto: "A face with gladness overspread, Soft smiles by human kindness bred."
clubs: Vice President Red and Blue Club

name: Gardner, Artimus Frank
motto: "He was a scholar, and a ripe and good one, Exceeding wise, fair spoken and persuading."

name: Beck, Karl Ross
motto: "The power of dealing with numbers is a kind of detached lever arrangement, but is usually found in a mighty good watch."

name: Haynes, John Willard
motto: "When I behold this, I sighed, and said within myself, surely mortal man is a broomstick."

name: Lindsey, Robert
motto: "Steady and industrious."

name: Kerstine, Isidor
motto: "Warp your face if it be sour, I can spare a smile from mine."
clubs: Distinction in Sophomore and Junior Mathematics

name: Bender, Charles Alexander
motto: "A faultless body and a blameless mind."

name: Blassingame, Charles D.
motto: "True as a needle to a pole, Or as a dial to the sun."

name: Boggan, Jeff Mitchener
motto: "He thinks too much; such men are dangerous in their wisdom."

name: Boykin, Solomon R.
motto: "Rises early, studies late. Works all day with book and slate."

name: Green, John W.
motto: "Linked sweetness, long drawn out."

name: Brewer, M. Irving
motto: "Live man, well man, dead man, stiff. Dig 'em up, cut 'em up, what's the dif? Humerus, tumorous, blood and gore. I'll be an M. D. in two years more."

name: Hentz, Roger Pou
motto: "We do not count years till we have nothing else to count."

name: Johnson, Lon C.
motto: "When a man marries his trouble begins."
major: M.D., B. S. George Robinson Christian College; L. L. George Robinson Christian College; Master of Accounts, Hill's Business College

name: Krone, Willie Frank
motto: "The beauty of my form is my constant joy."

name: Links, Isaac Simon
motto: "There is some ill a-brewing toward my rest, For I dream of money bags tonight."

name: McCarley, Thomas Randolph
motto: "He is complete in feature and in mind, With all good grace, to grace a gentleman."

name: McRae, Maury Holcombe
motto: "Maidens, beware! This lord has his eyes upon you."

name: Oats, Junius K.
motto: "Take him for all and all, you'll not find his like again."

name: Pound, Robert Earle
motto: "The time I've lost in woeing,  In watching and pursuing  The light that lies in woman's eyes,  Has been my heart's undoing."
major: M.D., B. S., University of Mississippi

name: Rush, Benjamin Clarence
motto: "Take your medicine like a man,"

name: Avent, Thomas Edison
motto: "When found, make a note of it."

name: Collum, James T.
motto: "Shortly his fortunes shall be lifted higher."

name: Dent, Albert Tatum Jr.
motto: "Great talkers are never great doers."

name: Duckworth, Frederick W.
motto: "None but himself can be his parallel."

name: Dyess, C. Rankin
motto: "Cheerfulness and content are famous preservers of good looks."

name: Hale, Ruben Aldridge
motto: "Another good farmer spoiled."

name: Hall, Linus Parker
motto: "Oh, bliss is mine, for into my joyless life a woman has come."

name: Lott, Gavin Ross
motto: "Minds that have nothing to confide, find little to perceive."

name: Middleton, Garland Whitfield
motto: "A fly sat upon the axle of a chariot and exclaimed, 'What a dust I do raise!'"

name: Mills, Ralph
motto: "A student, an athlete, and a man."

name: Rayner, Hugh Sennes
motto: "It is sometimes hard to believe that the Lord made all men in his own image."

name: Tatum, Howard Jack
motto: "As idle as a painted ship Upon a painted ocean."

name: Turner, Matthew Henry Jr.
motto: "He was not born to shame; Upon his brow shame is ashamed to sit. For 'tis the throne where honor may be crowned Safe monarch of the universal Earth."
